title: Preserve Agent Plugin Lifecycle Dependency Direction
forbids: root service capabilities or service-level models depending upward on sealed capability modules, provider code reaching current-main through governance module or binding imports instead of the neutral root dependency, module router bypass of module context, root router bypass of impl.router and module routers, service imports of concrete providers or controller implementation, module-port re-exports from the retired internal bucket, controller release/evidence mechanics outside resource bindings, a second production value consumer of the lifecycle service client, and any peer service client reachable from the lifecycle projection
why: The root declares ready capabilities without importing the sealed governance, packaging, releases, or vendors modules, and that sealed set grows monotonically as context-direction slices land. Governance owns current-main selection while providers consume only its neutral root observation contract. The service owns lifecycle semantics behind module-local router handlers and type-only resource protocols. The root composes module routers through impl.router. Runtime construction narrows through one exact CLI composition root, then through declared provider projections and exact binding facades.
remediate: Declare root capabilities and cross-module observation contracts under service/model, consume current-main from service/model/dependencies/current-main rather than governance modules or bindings, project capabilities into each module from module.ts, implement procedure handlers from the local module surface, compose module routers through impl.router, keep ports protocol/type-only, construct the lifecycle client only in apps/cli/src/lib/agent-plugins/service-runtime/client.ts, use type-only client dependencies elsewhere, keep concrete providers in declared CLI binding or provider-projection roots, and keep release/evidence mechanics in resource providers.
message: Lifecycle dependencies flow from root-owned capabilities and neutral cross-module observations into sealed module contexts and handlers, then through impl.router and one qualified CLI composition root to exact resource bindings.
